title: pelargonium × pudicum
intro: Pelargonium × pudicum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Dwarf Pelargonium, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen perennial plant native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pelargonium × pudicum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
